Adds an observe-only Security Matrix evaluator, heuristic tool classifier, audit-event builders, tests, documentation, and an exported adapter at the trusted tool policy boundary.

PR surface: Source +678, Tests +394, Docs +163. Total +1235 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review defect: invoke the new adapter for a plugin-owned tool without optional fact overrides, and source inspection shows the event is labeled as core-owned. There is no current-main product bug because the proposed feature is new and inert.

target.title: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.
- Adds a before-tool-call fact adapter and anchors it to the trusted tool policy boundary through an inert audit-event builder.
- Removes the unsupported `security.matrix.audit.enabled` placeholder so this PR does not reserve a parallel config surface.
- Removes the Security Matrix production files from the dead-code allowlist after adding the trusted-policy boundary import path.
- Hardens custom policy parsing so malformed decisions cannot weaken the default policy.

## Scope

This PR remains non-enforcing and observe-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution. The before-tool-call adapter only builds an audit event object from explicit boundary facts. Runtime diagnostic emission, canonical config, CLI display, and enforcement remain future work.

## Validation

Latest cleanup head: `d3617ce133ab12530d324a8961fedde83d38a99c`.

GitHub Actions have been triggered for the latest head. At the time this body was updated, CodeQL, CodeQL Critical Quality, OpenGrep PR Diff, CI, and Workflow Sanity were queued or pending.

Focused commands to run against this head:

```bash
pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts
pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/ src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

Earlier focused validation at prior head `d192028ab6cd7f962a7007f0f94c77d6b8b8e4aa` passed 28 tests plus lint/docs/whitespace checks. The branch has since been narrowed to remove the unsupported config placeholder, remove the global artifact Knip ignore, remove Security Matrix dead-code exemptions, clarify docs, and add capability fallback coverage.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluation produces deterministic audit decisions from actor, influence source, capability, approval state, and operator policy. The before-tool-call adapter builds the same audit-event shape from explicit boundary facts without emitting, blocking, or mutating execution. Malformed custom policy decisions are ignored instead of being treated as policy output.
- Runtime boundary addressed: The Security Matrix adapter is now imported from `src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts` through `createTrustedToolPolicySecurityMatrixAuditEvent`, so the model is anchored at the existing trusted before-tool-call policy boundary rather than existing only as allowlisted dead production code.
- Evidence after fix: The Security Matrix production files were removed from `KNIP_UNUSED_FILE_ALLOWLIST`, and the unsupported `security.matrix.audit.enabled` local config predicate was removed.
- Observed result after fix: Missing approval facts now remain `approvalState=none` instead of being labeled `not_required`, reducing the risk of misleading future audit records.
- What was not tested on this latest cleanup head from this session: Runtime hook emission, runtime enforcement, strict config schema adoption, CLI display, plugin UI, and live tool execution are intentionally not changed by this PR.

commands.review_summary:
The branch converts `docs/` into a Mintlify source tree, removes the current OpenClaw docs-sync bridge, and changes related build, navigation, docs-auth, proxy, CSP, audit, test, and dependency surfaces.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blockers: final-head source directly demonstrates publication removal, the credential-forwarding proxy path, and loss of the old auth route. The claimed successful Mintlify rendering itself lacks inspectable final-head evidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer must choose and coordinate the permanent publication, routing, auth-compatibility, and product-navigation boundary; contributor action is also required for real behavior proof and the concrete blockers.

target.title: docs: prepare ClawHub docs for Mintlify
target.body:
## Summary

- Converts `docs/` into the ClawHub Mintlify source tree with `docs/docs.json`, ClawHub branding, navbar, and `docs/index.md` as the home page.
- Removes the temporary OpenClaw docs-builder bridge, sync workflow dispatch, and generated-docs preview scripts.
- Adds a build guard so stale ignored `public/docs` output cannot be accidentally included in the app build.
- Keeps ClawHub app/email/CLI user-facing docs links on the current `https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/` host until the Mintlify `/docs` route is actually configured.

## Proof

- `bun run docs:check` -> passed (`success build validation passed`).
- `bunx vitest run src/__tests__/header.test.tsx src/__tests__/ui-design-contract.test.ts src/__tests__/skills-publish-route.test.tsx src/__tests__/plugins-publish-route.test.tsx src/__tests__/package-detail-route.test.tsx convex/packageInspectorNode.test.ts convex/lib/emails.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.test.ts` -> 9 files / 323 tests passed.
- `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub test:src -- src/cli/commands/packages.test.ts` -> 1 file / 66 tests passed.
- `bun run ci:static` -> passed.
- `VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid bun run build` -> passed.
- Local Mintlify preview rendered the Quickstart page with title `Quickstart - ClawHub`, ClawHub logo, search, and the ClawHub navbar link.

## Review Notes

- I intentionally did not switch production/user-facing links to `https://clawhub.ai/docs` in this PR. Live route probing showed `clawhub.ai/docs` currently returns 404, while `docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub` is still live.
- Remaining cutover work: connect/configure the Mintlify project for the repo `docs/` directory, configure host-level routing for `https://clawhub.ai/docs`, then switch app/email/CLI docs links to the canonical ClawHub docs URL.
- Ask Molty is not restored by this Mintlify-source PR; that needs a separate Mintlify-compatible assistant/auth decision before claiming the original Molty sign-in proof goal.
- Autoreview found and we accepted one earlier P1 about switching links before `/docs` routing existed; this revision fixes that by keeping public links on the current docs host. The final rerun of the nested autoreview process hung after independently rerunning `docs:check`, app build, live route probes, and route/config inspection, so I interrupted the stuck review process and relied on the explicit verification above.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
